status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task. Only one allowed.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

milestones.M1: First trivial fork change visible end-to-end.
milestones.M2: Architecture absorbed: can locate any feature in <5 min.
milestones.M3: First real product change shipped behind a fork feature flag.

features.title: Harness baseline: cargo check passes from a clean session
features.user_visible_behavior: Running `./init.sh` from a fresh shell completes successfully with `cargo check` exit 0.
verification: Open a fresh terminal.
cd /Users/troye/Project/warp
Run `./init.sh`.
Confirm 'Standard start command: $ cargo run' is printed at the end with no errors.
evidence: Session 002 ran `cargo check` (default-members) in 2m12s, exit 0, 44 unused-variable warnings, 0 errors. See progress.md Session 002.
features.notes: Hardened init.sh exports ~/.cargo/bin and uses `cargo check` (not --workspace) to avoid the command-signatures-v2 Node toolchain dependency.

features.title: Launch warp-oss binary and reach the main window
features.user_visible_behavior: Running `cargo run --bin warp-oss` opens a Warp terminal window on macOS.
verification: cd /Users/troye/Project/warp
Run `cargo run --bin warp-oss`.
Wait for first compilation (5-10 minutes cold).
Confirm a terminal window appears.
Type `echo hello` and press Enter — output should appear in a block.
Capture a screenshot or note the window title and PID.
evidence: 2026-04-30 Session 003: `cargo build --bin warp-oss` finished in 4.06s (incremental on top of Session 002 cargo check), 0 errors, 44 unused-variable warnings.
Launched as PID 50148, forked terminal-server child PID 50482 (`warp-oss terminal-server --parent-pid=50148`) — confirms Warp's normal multi-process model.
macOS visible-process check via `osascript ... visible is true` lists `warp-oss` — GUI window is on screen.
features.notes: Use the oss binary (no telemetry, no autoupdater, no crash reporting). If linking fails, see DEVELOPMENT.md §5.1. Operator should manually verify the window is interactive (type a command, see a block) — automated check confirmed only that the window exists.

features.title: First trivial fork change visible end-to-end
features.user_visible_behavior: After a code edit, the Warp window shows a visible difference (e.g., changed window title, default theme color, or a banner string).
verification: Pick one user-visible string or constant in app/src/ (do NOT change behavior).
Edit it, mark with `// FORK: smoke-test edit`.
Run `cargo check -p warp` (where warp = app crate) — must pass.
Run `cargo run --bin warp-oss` — observe the change.
Revert the change and confirm window goes back to original.
evidence: 2026-04-30 Session 003: edited app/src/bin/oss.rs CFBundleDisplayName from 'WarpOss' to 'WarpOss-Fork' with `<!-- FORK: smoke-test edit (M1-001) -->` marker.
Incremental rebuild: `cargo build --bin warp-oss` finished in 30.03s (only `app` crate recompiled — proves fast-iteration loop works).
Re-launched binary as PID 60168 with terminal-server child PID 60579, GUI listed in macOS visible processes, stdout/stderr empty.
Verified embedded Info.plist via `strings target/debug/warp-oss | grep WarpOss` → output contains both 'WarpOss-Fork' and the FORK comment, confirming the change reached the running binary.
Reverted: `git diff app/src/bin/oss.rs` → empty; `git status` shows only the 8 new harness files, no source code changes.
features.notes: Goal achieved: edit→compile→run→verify→revert loop works in ~30s of incremental build time. CFBundleDisplayName affects the .app bundle Dock title; when running the bare debug binary it's not user-visible in the window chrome, so we verified via `strings` instead. For a more visually obvious smoke test next time, change a string actually rendered in the UI (e.g., a banner or default placeholder text).

features.title: Pick a focus area and absorb its specs
features.user_visible_behavior: Operator can name the 3-5 files most likely to change for the chosen area, and the relevant specs/APP-* folders.
verification: Decide focus area (UI / AI / terminal / drive / fork).
List the 3-5 most relevant `app/src/<dir>` modules in this feature's `notes`.
List the 2-3 most relevant `specs/APP-*` folders in this feature's `notes`.
Skim each listed spec and write a one-line summary in `notes`.
features.notes: Focus area is currently 'undecided — explore mode'. Update this entry once direction is chosen.

features.title: First real product change behind a fork feature flag
features.user_visible_behavior: A new behavior is implemented, gated behind a Cargo feature added to the fork. Disabling the flag returns the app to upstream behavior.
verification: Replace this entry with a concrete spec (PRODUCT.md style) once chosen.
Add a Cargo feature in app/Cargo.toml gating all new code paths.
`cargo check --no-default-features` must still pass (proof the flag isolates).
`cargo run --bin warp-oss` with the flag enabled shows the new behavior.
`cargo run --bin warp-oss --no-default-features --features <minimal>` shows old behavior.

features.title: Exercise upstream sync workflow once
features.user_visible_behavior: Operator can rebase the fork onto upstream/master, resolve any conflicts, and end with a green `cargo check`.
verification: Add upstream remote: `git remote add upstream https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp.git`.
Fetch: `git fetch upstream`.
Inspect: `git log --oneline master..upstream/master | head -20`.
Rebase: `git rebase upstream/master` (resolve conflicts using .claude/skills/resolve-merge-conflicts/SKILL.md).
Run `cargo check`. Must be green.
Run `cargo run --bin warp-oss`. Must launch.
features.notes: Don't actually push the rebase; this is a dry run to learn the conflict surface.
